generatedSentence: Woken from a deep sojourn, the deject man pulled his wicker blanket closer, feeling beaten by the wars fought in his brainwashed past, a distant habanera mocking his barhopping memories; glimpsing winners on distant walkways from the bottom of his trolleybus, his vulnerable vest offered no warmth, his wallet gone, orientating himself to another lucky weekend, searching for a keyhole, a keyword for hope, as a wave of despair washed over the iron drainage. The awesome musketeer, an earthling veteran of countless battleships, wore a dirty jumpsuit, his backhand blocking an icicle shard, as he navigated the greenhouse outskirts, a diligent ornithology student playing with his pet kakapo, unaware his founder's empire was being oiled by a viceroyalty plagued by pestilence; his multitasking mind, however, sensed a creeping paranoia, like cobwebs in the holiest corners of the forest, a phantom firefly leading him into a deep inch of nothingness. Unbroken by a lifetime of slavery, the authentic hunchback occupant, a virgin, studied newspaper intel by fluorescence, his eyeline following the uncommon repetition of a werewolf's howl from the djebel, a whacky raptor's shadow blocking the moon; he prepared ammunition from his apothecary, unsure of the approaching boundary and the imminent banking foreclosure, knowing his intuition was a stunning solution, but fearful of the heartbreak ahead. With unlimited jujitsu moves and a strategic hairclip, he executed a perfect knock out punch, sending the icefish-like villain back to jail, ending the leasing and layoff cycle of the deep line; a vital herb, a blackberry, offered an ultimate intel boost, a cabernet-colored jetpack ready for liftoff, racing towards the united future, ensuring no more prison sojourns for these pilgrims.
panelSentences: Woken from a deep sojourn, the deject man pulled his wicker blanket closer, feeling beaten by the wars fought in his brainwashed past, a distant habanera mocking his barhopping memories; glimpsing winners on distant walkways from the bottom of his trolleybus, his vulnerable vest offered no warmth, his wallet gone, orientating himself to another lucky weekend, searching for a keyhole, a keyword for hope, as a wave of despair washed over the iron drainage.

The awesome musketeer, an earthling veteran of countless battleships, wore a dirty jumpsuit, his backhand blocking an icicle shard, as he navigated the greenhouse outskirts, a diligent ornithology student playing with his pet kakapo, unaware his founder's empire was being oiled by a viceroyalty plagued by pestilence; his multitasking mind, however, sensed a creeping paranoia, like cobwebs in the holiest corners of the forest, a phantom firefly leading him into a deep inch of nothingness.

Unbroken by a lifetime of slavery, the authentic hunchback occupant, a virgin, studied newspaper intel by fluorescence, his eyeline following the uncommon repetition of a werewolf's howl from the djebel, a whacky raptor's shadow blocking the moon; he prepared ammunition from his apothecary, unsure of the approaching boundary and the imminent banking foreclosure, knowing his intuition was a stunning solution, but fearful of the heartbreak ahead.

With unlimited jujitsu moves and a strategic hairclip, he executed a perfect knock out punch, sending the icefish-like villain back to jail, ending the leasing and layoff cycle of the deep line; a vital herb, a blackberry, offered an ultimate intel boost, a cabernet-colored jetpack ready for liftoff, racing towards the united future, ensuring no more prison sojourns for these pilgrims.
rawPanelSentences: Woken from a deep sojourn, the deject man pulled his wicker blanket closer, feeling beaten by the wars fought in his brainwashed past, a distant habanera mocking his barhopping memories; glimpsing winners on distant walkways from the bottom of his trolleybus, his vulnerable vest offered no warmth, his wallet gone, orientating himself to another lucky weekend, searching for a keyhole, a keyword for hope, as a wave of despair washed over the iron drainage.

The awesome musketeer, an earthling veteran of countless battleships, wore a dirty jumpsuit, his backhand blocking an icicle shard, as he navigated the greenhouse outskirts, a diligent ornithology student playing with his pet kakapo, unaware his founder's empire was being oiled by a viceroyalty plagued by pestilence; his multitasking mind, however, sensed a creeping paranoia, like cobwebs in the holiest corners of the forest, a phantom firefly leading him into a deep inch of nothingness.

Unbroken by a lifetime of slavery, the authentic hunchback occupant, a virgin, studied newspaper intel by fluorescence, his eyeline following the uncommon repetition of a werewolf's howl from the djebel, a whacky raptor's shadow blocking the moon; he prepared ammunition from his apothecary, unsure of the approaching boundary and the imminent banking foreclosure, knowing his intuition was a stunning solution, but fearful of the heartbreak ahead.

With unlimited jujitsu moves and a strategic hairclip, he executed a perfect knock out punch, sending the icefish-like villain back to jail, ending the leasing and layoff cycle of the deep line; a vital herb, a blackberry, offered an ultimate intel boost, a cabernet-colored jetpack ready for liftoff, racing towards the united future, ensuring no more prison sojourns for these pilgrims.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. The protagonist is a stoic space musketeer, wearing a tattered, patched jumpsuit and a glowing green energy vest. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A lone figure, hunched, sitting on a dilapidated wicker bench inside a rusty trolleybus, looking out at rain-streaked windows, a deep sense of dejection visible, extreme wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The musketeer, mid-air, grappling with a large, reptilian raptor in a bioluminescent greenhouse, a small kakapo perched on his shoulder, dynamic mid-shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A close-up of the musketeer's determined face, lit by the eerie fluorescence of a holographic newspaper, a single hairclip glinting, intense portrait shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The musketeer, wearing a sleek cabernet jetpack, soaring into a united cityscape with other pilgrims, the city skyline glowing with neon, triumphant wide shot.

definitions.definition: (with "the") The deep part of a lake, sea, etc.

definitions.definition: (of a color) Highly saturated.

To cast down.

definitions.definition: To put in motion or action; to arouse; to excite.
To be excited or roused up; to be stirred up from a dormant, torpid, or inactive state; to be active.
To lay out a body prior to burial in order to allow family and friends to pay their last respects.

Organized, large-scale, armed conflict between countries or between national, ethnic, or other sizeable groups, usually involving the engagement of military forces.

Armed forces.

To carry on, as a contest; to wage.

A style of music from Cuba.
A dance performed to this music.
A common, inexpensive metal, silvery grey when untarnished, that rusts, is attracted by magnets, and is used in making steel.
A metallic chemical element having atomic number 26 and symbol Fe.
Any material, not a steel, predominantly made of elemental iron.

definitions.definition: A tool or appliance made of metal, which is heated and then used to transfer heat to something else; most often a thick piece of metal fitted with a handle and having a flat, roughly triangular bottom, which is heated and used to press wrinkles from clothing, and now usually containing an electrical heating apparatus.
(usually plural, irons) Shackles.
A firearm, either a long gun or a handgun.
A dark shade of the colour/color silver.

Used as a symbol of great strength or toughness, or to signify a very strong or tough material.

Strong (as of will), inflexible.

The free-throw lane together with the circle surrounding the free-throw line; key.
A gravitational keyhole.

definitions.definition: (of an inheritance or a trust fund) To devolve upon the person currently entitled when a prior interest has ended.

(of a set) The set of points in the closure of a set S, not belonging to the interior of that set.

definitions.definition: (gerund of play) An occasion on which something, such as a song or show, is played.
One who is stooped or hunched over.
A deformed upper spinal column in the shape of a hump in the back.
A person with kyphosis, a spinal deformity that causes a hunched over appearance.
Overwhelming mental anguish or grief, especially that caused by loss or disappointment
Having, or being full of, holes

definitions.definition: Dedicated to a religious purpose or a god.

definitions.definition: Revered in a religion.
Morally perfect or flawless, or nearly so.
definitions.example: My grandmother is a very holy woman.

definitions.definition: Separated or set apart from (something unto something or someone else).
Set apart or dedicated for a specific purpose, or for use by a single entity or person.
Used as an intensifier in various interjections.

definitions.definition: The emission of light (or other electromagnetic radiation) by a material when stimulated by the absorption of radiation or of a subatomic particle.
The light so emitted.
One who founds or establishes (especially said of a company, project, organisation, state)

definitions.definition: Someone for whose parents one has no data.
To make very dirty; to cover in filth.
Covered with filth; very dirty.
Obscene or offensive.
Very unpleasant or disagreeable.
A dense uncultivated tract of trees and undergrowth, larger than woods.
Any dense collection or amount.

definitions.definition: A defined area of land set aside in England as royal hunting ground or for other privileged use; all such areas.
A graph with no cycles; i.e., a graph made up of trees.
A group of domains that are managed as a unit.
The colour forest green.
To cover an area with trees.
The proceeding, by a creditor, to regain property or other collateral following a default on mortgage payments
The absence of a symbolic father for a fatherless child, as a cause for psychosis.
To deal with a bank or financial institution, or for an institution to provide financial services to a client.

definitions.definition: To put into a bank.
definitions.example: I'm going to bank the money.

definitions.definition: To conceal in the rectum for use in prison.

definitions.definition: To roll or incline laterally in order to turn.
To cause (an aircraft) to bank.
To form into a bank or heap, to bank up.

definitions.definition: To cover the embers of a fire with ashes in order to retain heat.
To raise a mound or dike about; to enclose, defend, or fortify with a bank; to embank.
To pass by the banks of.
To provide additional power for a train ascending a bank (incline) by attaching another locomotive.
(order and arrangement) To arrange or order in a row.
The business of managing a bank.
The occupation of managing or working in a bank.
A horizontal turn.
A mechanical component to prevent vibration in a timepiece, etc.
The practice of assisting a train up a steep incline (called a bank) with another locomotive at the rear.
An institution or social practice of owning human beings as property, especially for use as forced laborers.
A condition of servitude endured by a slave.
A condition in which one is captivated or subjugated, as by greed or drugs.
The act or an instance of repeating or being repeated.
: The act of performing a single, controlled exercise motion. A group of repetitions is a set.
Uncertain

definitions.definition: Unstable or precarious
definitions.example: unsure footing

definitions.definition: Whole, not divided into parts.
definitions.example: After the vase had fallen down the flight of stairs we were amazed to find it still unbroken.

definitions.definition: Of a horse, not tamed.
definitions.example: There is something majestic about the spirit of an unbroken mustang as it runs wild across the prairie.

definitions.definition: Continuous, without interruption.
definitions.example: The team's unbroken winning streak was a record.

definitions.definition: The branch of zoology that deals with the scientific study of birds.
A person who occupies an office or a position.
definitions.example: I cannot say the same of the current occupant of the position

definitions.definition: A person who occupies a place.
The owner or tenant of a property.
The area surrounding a city or town; suburb.
definitions.example: He lived on the outskirts of Paris for a time.

definitions.definition: A publication, usually published daily or weekly and usually printed on cheap, low-quality paper, containing news and other articles.
A quantity of or one of the types of paper on which newspapers are printed.
To cover with newspaper.
definitions.example: She newspapered one end of the room before painting the bookcase.

definitions.definition: To engage in the business of journalism (usually used only in the gerund, newspapering)
definitions.example: He newspapered his way through the South on the sports beat, avoiding dry towns.

definitions.definition: To harass in newspaper articles.
definitions.example: He was newspapered out of public life.

definitions.definition: The horizontal portion of the face that includes the eyes.
definitions.example: In the shadows above my eyeline, in the corner of the room, I saw something move.

definitions.definition: A stripe on each side of the head of an animal that includes the eye, and is of a different colour from the rest of the head; in particular such a stripe on each side of the head of some birds.
A large flightless parrot, Strigops habroptilus, with greenish plumage, that is nocturnal and native to New Zealand.
A person with long experience of a particular activity.
A group, animal, etc. with long experience of a particular activity.
A person who has served in the armed forces, especially an old soldier who has seen long service; also called a war veteran to distinguish from veterans that weren't in armed conflict.
Having had long experience, practice, or service.
Of or relating to former members of the military armed forces, especially those who served during wartime.
A person who has never had sexual intercourse, or sometimes, one who has never engaged in any sexual activity at all.
(early Christian Church) a woman noted for religious piety who has never been married.

definitions.definition: Any of several species of gossamer-winged butterflies of the family Lycaenidae.
A female insect producing eggs from which young are hatched, though there has been no fecundation by a male; a parthenogenetic insect.
(usually not comparable) In a state of virginity; chaste, not having had sexual intercourse.
Of a physical object, untouched.
Not yet cultivated, explored, or exploited by humans or humans of certain civilizations.

definitions.definition: Of olive oil, obtained by mechanical means, so that the oil is not altered.
(usually not comparable) Of mixed drinks, not containing alcohol.

definitions.definition: A hill, a mountain (especially in the Middle East or North Africa).
Zany; eccentric
A person who is transformed or can transform into a wolf or a wolflike human, often said to transform during a full moon.
A person who makes and provides/sells drugs and/or medicines.
A drugstore or pharmacy.
A glass jar similar to those once used for medicine.
Articles used in charging firearms and ordnance of all kinds; as powder, balls, shot, shells, percussion caps, rockets, etc.
Military stores, or provisions of all kinds for attack or defense.

To supply with ammunition.
Of the same origin as claimed; genuine.

definitions.definition: Conforming to reality and therefore worthy of trust, reliance, or belief.

definitions.definition: (of a Gregorian mode) Having the final as the lowest note of the mode.
Authoritative

Extreme, irrational distrust of others.
A clip to hold back the hair.
Any green, leafy plant, or parts thereof, used to flavour or season food.
A plant whose roots, leaves or seeds, etc. are used in medicine.

definitions.definition: Marijuana.
A plant whose stem is not woody and does not persist beyond each growing season
Grass; herbage.
To fish through a hole cut into ice.
Any member of notothenioid family Channichthyidae; these Antarctic fishes are named for the lack of hemoglobin in their blood
The noodlefish (family Salangidae in order Osmeriformes).
Intelligence (secret information)

definitions.definition: A stroke made across the chest from the off-hand side to the racquet hand side; a stroke during which the back of the hand faces the shot.
Handwriting that leans to the left
(Ultimate Frisbee) the standard throw; a throw during which the disc begins on the off-hand side and travels across the chest to be released from the opposite side.
The reverse side of the human hand.
The hand towards the back of the board.
To execute a backhand stroke or throw
To slap with the back of one's hand
(handwriting) Of any left slanting handwriting
(of strokes or throws) In the backhand style
Of a play that uses the back side of the hockey stick

The soft fruit borne by this shrub, formed of a black (when ripe) cluster of drupelets.
The blackcurrant.
To gather or forage for blackberries.
To fill (something) so that it is not possible to pass.

definitions.definition: To prevent (something or someone) from passing.

definitions.definition: To impede an opponent.

definitions.definition: To specify the positions and movements of the actors.

definitions.definition: To hit with a block.
To play a block shot.
To disable communication via telephone, instant messaging, etc., with an undesirable someone.

definitions.definition: To wait.

definitions.definition: To stretch or mould (a knitted item, a hat, etc.) into the desired shape.

definitions.definition: To shape or sketch out roughly.
The act by which something is blocked; an obstruction.

definitions.definition: The precise movement and positioning of actors on a stage in order to facilitate the performance of a play, ballet, film or opera (originally planned using miniature blocks).
Blocks used to support (a building, etc.) temporarily.
A feature that prevents a keyboard from registering multiple simultaneous keypresses that would cause ghosting.
A homogeneous mixture, which may be liquid, gas or solid, formed by dissolving one or more substances.
An act, plan or other means, used or proposed, to solve a problem.
The answer to a problem.
A product, service or suite thereof, especially software.
Satisfaction of a claim or debt.
The act of dissolving, especially of a solid by a fluid; dissolution.
The crisis of a disease.
To treat with a solution.
To incapacitate; especially by inducing disorientation or unconsciousness.

Having an effect that stuns.

definitions.definition: Exceptionally beautiful or attractive.

definitions.definition: Amazing; wonderfully good.

definitions.definition: To move or drive at high speed; to hurry or speed.

To demolish; to level to the ground.

definitions.definition: Exceedingly, exceptionally.
Limitless or without bounds; unrestricted

Brazilian jiu-jitsu, a related art, derived from judo.
Precision maneuvering.
A place or institution for the confinement of persons held in lawful custody or detention, especially for minor offenses or with reference to some future judicial proceeding.
Confinement in a jail.

To imprison.

A lie; the act of lying, falsehood.

A period of time when someone is unavailable for work.
A short pass that has been rolled in front of another player for them to kick.
A path through two or more points (compare ‘segment’); a continuous mark, including as made by a pen; any path, curved or straight.

definitions.definition: A rope, cord, string, or thread, of any thickness.
A hose or pipe, of any size.

definitions.definition: Direction, path.

definitions.definition: The wire connecting one telegraphic station with another, a telephone or internet cable between two points: a telephone or network connection.

definitions.definition: A clothesline.
A letter, a written form of communication.

definitions.definition: A connected series of public conveyances, as a roadbed or railway track; and hence, an established arrangement for forwarding merchandise, etc.

definitions.definition: A trench or rampart, or the non-physical demarcation of the extent of the territory occupied by specified forces.

A long tape or ribbon marked with units for measuring; a tape measure.

That which was measured by a line, such as a field or any piece of land set apart; hence, allotted place of abode.
A threadlike crease or wrinkle marking the face, hand, or body; hence, a characteristic mark.
Lineament; feature; figure (of one's body).

A more-or-less straight sequence of people, objects, etc., either arranged as a queue or column and often waiting to be processed or dealt with, or arranged abreast of one another in a row (and contrasted with a column), as in a military formation.

definitions.definition: The regular infantry of an army, as distinguished from militia, guards, volunteer corps, cavalry, artillery etc.

Course of conduct, thought, occupation, or policy; method of argument; department of industry, trade, or intellectual activity.

Proper relative position or adjustment (of parts, not as to design or proportion, but with reference to smooth working).

To measure.
To strike or bump (someone or something) out.

definitions.definition: To render unconscious, as by a blow to the head.

definitions.definition: To put to sleep.

definitions.definition: To exhaust.

definitions.definition: To complete, especially in haste; knock off.

definitions.definition: To lose the scent of hounds in fox-hunting.
